title: Erycina pumilio
taxonomy: Erycina pumilio is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification is further divided into the subfamily Epidendroideae, tribe Maxillarieae, and genus Erycina. The specific classification of Erycina pumilio is: Erycina pumilio (Lindl.) Hoehne. Its exact classification is still being studied and may be subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Erycina pumilio, also known as the "Peach Flower" or "Peach Orchid", is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erycina pumilio include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
